title: Chamaecytisus heuffelii
intro: Chamaecytisus heuffelii, also known as Heuffel's broom, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae, native to Central Europe and the Balkan Peninsula. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential use in treating gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems. Bioactive constituents of Chamaecytisus heuffelii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Heuffel's broom include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Chamaecytisus heuffelii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the genus Chamaecytisus and the subfamily Faboideae. This plant is native to Europe, specifically in the mountainous regions of the continent. Chamaecytisus heuffelii is also known as Hungarian bitter pea and is a type of shrub that grows up to a height of approximately 2-3 meters. It is a part of the Legume family which includes pea, bean, and lentil species.

It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation. constituents: Chamaecytisus heuffelii contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. These compounds have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
